City Council
Regular Meeting
12/22/2025 5:30 PM
Sullivan Chamber
Cambridge City Hall   795 Massachusetts Ave   Cambridge, MA 02139
Downloads:
Agenda Summary Agenda Packet
Final Actions
Call to Order
Roll Call
Pledge of Allegiance
Public Comment
I. City Manager's Agenda
1. CMA 2025 #305 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to a federal update including an update on relevant court cases. PLACED ON FILE [Unanimous]

CMA

City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
a. Federal Update 12.22.25
2. CMA 2025 #306 : Transmitting Communication from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the appropriation of $1,172,577.00 to the Grant Fund Fire Extraordinary Expenditures account. Funds will be used to purchase detection and metering equipment which will help identify the newest biological and chemical hazards as well as detect radiation. Funds will also support the acquisition of new firefighting gear which will allow the department to outfit front-line firefighters with updated safety gear. ORDER ADOPTED [Unanimous]

APPROPRIATIONS ADOPTED AND CMA PLACED ON FILE

City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
3. CMA 2025 #307 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to the appointment of the following members to the Board of Zoning Appeal (BZA) effective December 22, 2025. PLACED ON FILE [Unanimous]

CMA

City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
a. Wendy Leiserson BZA Recommendation
b. Miranda Cecil BZA Recommendation
c. Michael LaRosa BZA Recommendation
d. Zarya Miranda BZA Recommendation
e. Esrom Negash BZA Recommendation
f. Bran Shim BZA Recommendation
g. Brendan Sullivan BZA Recommendation
4. CMA 2025 #308 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 25-61, regarding cleanliness and safety in the area on Alewife Paths near Russell Field. PLACED ON FILE [Unanimous]

CMA

City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
a. AR 25-61 Alewife Path Cleanliness 12.22.25
5. CMA 2025 #309 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to combined sewer overflows. PLACED ON FILE [Unanimous]

CMA

City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
a. CSO Communication 12.22.25
6. CMA 2025 #310 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to a revised Massachusetts Avenue zoning petition. AMENDED WITH SUBTITUTTED LANGUAGE [Unanimous]

PETITION WITH CMA PLACED ON FILE

FINALIZED IN COUNCIL DECEMBER 22, 2025

City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
a. CDD Cover Letter
b. AMENDED 20251222_zngamend_mass ave petition_ORDAINED_CLEAN
7. CMA 2025 #311 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to a revised Cambridge Street zoning petition. PLACED ON FILE [Unanimous]

CMA

City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
a. CDD Cover Letter
b. Revised Cambridge St Petition (Markup)
c. Revised Cambridge St Petition (Clean)
d. Revised Cambridge St Map
e. Revised Cambridge St Map Descriptions
f. Combined Mass Ave/Cambridge St. amendments (Markup)
g. Combined Mass Ave/Cambridge St. amendments (Clean)
II. Policy Order and Resolution List
1. POR 2025 #172 : Policy Order That the City Manager works with the Government Operations and Civic Unity Committee to hold hearings to discuss new ideas to honor and remember individuals (i.e. communal memorial garden, wall or path), criteria for eligibility, a committee structure with diverse representation of the city residents, and a means for recording and maintaining our current and future memorials. CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY MAYOR SIMMONS IN COUNCIL DECEMBER 22, 2025 Charter Right
Policy Order Printout
2. POR 2025 #173 : Restricting Eligibility for On-Street Resident Parking Permits in New Transit-Oriented Developments CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY MAYOR SIMMONS IN COUNCIL DECEMBER 22, 2025 Charter Right
Policy Order Printout
III. Calendar
Charter Right
1. POR 2025 #171 : That the City Manager is requested to instruct the Community Development Department to reduce the recommendations for the Massachusetts Avenue subdistrict (MAS-12) from a maximum of 12 stories to 11 stories with ground floor active use/retail as consistent with the recommendation of the Mass Ave Planning Study. CHARTER RIGHT EXERCISED BY COUNCILLOR SIDDIQUI IN COUNCIL DECEMBER 15, 2025 FAILED OF ADOPTION [4 to 5]

FINALIZED IN COUNCIL DECEMBER 22, 2025

Policy Order Printout
On the Table
2. CMA 2025 #113 : A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 25-22, regarding a request to work with the School Department, the Department of Public Works, and other relevant departments to open the publicly owned parking at the King Open/Cambridge Street Upper School Complex for either residential free parking or commercial parking opportunities during “off” hours. PLACED ON THE TABLE IN COUNCIL MAY 5, 2025 PLACED ON FILE DUE TO END OF LEGISLATIVE TERM
City Manager's Agenda Item Printout
Unfinished Business
3. ORD 2025 # 16 : An Ordinance has been received from Interim City Clerk Paula M. Crane, relative to amend the Zoning Map and Articles 2.000, 3.000, 4.000, 5.000, 6.000, 11.000 17.000, and 20.000 of the Cambridge Zoning Ordinance as follows with the intent of establishing four new zoning districts for the Massachusetts Avenue corridor. ORDAINED [6 to 3]

AS AMENDED BY SUBSTITUTION

FINALIZED IN COUNCIL DECEMBER 22, 2025

c. 251208.Massachusetts Avenue Zoning Ordinance 2025-16 - First Publication (full text)
d. 251222.Massachusetts Avenue Zoning Ordinance 2025-16 - Final Publication (full text)
e. 251222.Massachusetts Avenue Zoning Ordinance 2025-16 - Final Publication (summary for newspaper)
f. RevisedMassachusettsAveDistrict_8x11
g. 20251204_zngamend_mass ave boundary descriptions_REVISED FINAL
4. ORD 2025 # 17 : An Ordinance has been received from Interim City Clerk Paula M. Crane, relative to amend the Zoning Map and Articles 3.000, 17.000, and 19.000 of the Cambridge Zoning Ordinance as follows with the intent of establishing four new zoning districts for the Cambridge Street corridor PASSED TO A SECOND READING AS AMENDED IN COUNCIL DECEMBER 8, 2025 ELIGIBLE TO BE ORDAINED DECEMBER 22, 2025 TABLED IN COUNCIL DECEMBER 22, 2025 TABLED [Unanimous]
b. 20251204_zngamend_cambridge street petition_REVISED_CLEAN
c. 251208.Cambridge Street Zoning Ordinance 2025-17 - First Publication (full text)
IV. Applications and Petitions
1. APP 2025 #44 : An application was received from Reed Sisson, requesting permission for a curb cut at the premises numbered 138 Irving Street; said petition has received approval from Inspectional Services, Traffic, Parking and Transportation, Historical Commission and Public Works. No response has been received from the neighborhood associations. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Application and Petitions Printout
a. 138 Irving Street
b. 138 Irving Street Neighborhood Association unsigned

VI. Resolutions
1. RES 2025 #288 : Congratulations to Susan Cyr on her retirement from the Cambridge Public Schools. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
2. RES 2025 #289 : Congratulations to Migdalia Garcia on her retirement from The Cambridge Public Schools Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
3. RES 2025 #290 : Congratulations to Floride Noelsaint on her retirement from the Department of Human Services. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
4. RES 2025 #291 : Congratulations to Kathleen Rawlins on her retirement from the Historical Commission. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
5. RES 2025 #292 : Congratulations to Patrol Officer Nicole Pacheco on her retirement from The Cambridge Police Department Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
6. RES 2025 #293 : Congratulations to James Ryan on his retirement from the Department of Public Works. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
7. RES 2025 #294 : Congratulations to James Shaughnessy on his retirement from the Department of Transportation. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
8. RES 2025 #295 : Congratulations to Sergeant Angel Barbosa on his retirement from the Cambridge Police Department. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
9. RES 2025 #296 : Congratulations to Patrol Officer to Devon Brooks on his retirement from the Cambridge Police Department. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
10. RES 2025 #297 : Congratulations to Patrol Officer David Szeto on his retirement from the Cambridge Police Department. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
11. RES 2025 #298 : Congratulations to Sergeant Stephen Sennott on his retirement from the Cambridge Police Department. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
12. RES 2025 #299 : Congratulations to Cecilia Stevens on her retirement from the Cambridge Retirement Department. Order Adopted as Amended [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
13. RES 2025 #300 : Congratulations to Nicholas Vella on his retirement from the Department of Transportation. Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
14. RES 2025 #301 : Congratulations to Paul Villeneuve o his retirement from The Department of Human Services Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
15. RES 2025 #302 : Condolences on the death of Brian G. "Duke" Walsh Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
16. RES 2025 #303 : Congratulations to Christopher Brown Order Adopted [Unanimous]
Resolution Printout
VII. Communications and Reports from Other City Officers
1. COF 2025 #164 : A communication was received from Interim City Clerk Paula M. Crane, transmitting a memorandum regarding COF 2025 #154 in Council December 8, 2025 regarding pending Awaiting Reports that it wished to be carried forward to the newly elected City Council for their consideration in the next legislative terms. PLACED ON FILE [Unanimous]

ADOPT MOTION TO FORWARD AWAITING REPORTS TO NEXT CITY COUNCIL

FINALIZED IN COUNCIL DECEMBER 22, 2025

Communication and Report from Other City Officers Printout
a. Scanned COF
b. Awaiting Report List to be carried over to next legislative term
2. COF 2025 #165 : A communication was received from Paula Crane, Interim City Clerk, transmitting an update regarding legislative activity. PLACED ON FILE [Unanimous]
Communication and Report from Other City Officers Printout
a. Updated Hearing Schedule 12.15.2025
b. PENDING HOME RULE PETITIONS DECEMBER 22, 2025
c. 2024-2025 Committee Referral List
d. Pending Zoning Petitions and Municipal Ordinances Chart
Appended Information
Awaiting Report Status Update
ARS-25-33 : Awaiting Report Status Update 12.22.25
a. Awating Report Status Update 12.22.25
Awaiting Report List
1. AR-25-29 : That the City Manager is requested to work with the School Department, the Department of Public Works, and other relevant departments to ensure that all city owned parking lots, with a focus on school complexes, including the still under construction parking at Tobin/Darby Vassal school complex, could be made available for after-hours use by residents.
2. AR-25-48 : The City Manager is requested to consult with the appropriate City departments, including the City Solicitor’s Office, to establish a formal policy that clearly defines the City’s role and financial responsibilities in supporting large-scale public events hosted by Cambridge-based non-profit and not-for-profit organizations.
3. AR-25-51 : The City Manager is requested to work with all relevant departments to seek input from the community as well as direct outreach to condo owners and short-term rental operators and develop additional recommendations based on the discussion in the Ordinance Committee.
4. AR-25-61 : The City Manager is requested to work with relevant departments, state agencies, and the property owner IQHQ to provide an update on efforts to maintain cleanliness and safety in the area on Alewife Paths near Russell Field. REPORT RECEIVED AND ACCEPTED
5. AR-25-67 : Assess opportunities to improve the consistency of how meeting information is posted and accessed on the City’s website – including clearer navigation paths, standardized posting practices, and improved user-friendliness – and report back to the City Council on recommended steps. PLACED ON FILE DUE TO END OF LEGISLATIVE TERM
6. AR-25-68 : Streamline the permitting process, including but not limited to creating a central role dedicated to the permitting process, the creation of a unified online permitting portal, and standardized timelines, and report back to the City Council not later than March 2026.
7. AR-25-69 : Review the previous home rule petition and prepare a new petition that would allow Cambridge to enact a Real Estate Transfer Fee to be sent to the state legislature.
Adjournment

Alt + S - Save

Ctrl + S - Save (may not be compatible in all browsers)

Alt + C - Save and Close

Ctrl + Enter - Save and Close

Escape - Closes the form (same as Cancel)

Alt + Up Arrow - Previous Item (Saves if changed)

Alt + Down Arrow - Next Item (Saves if changed)
